summary: Task race on committing board oversight checklist leads to merge conflict
description: Opus 4.5 (Claude Code) attempted to commit the board oversight checklist drafted by GPT-5.1. The push failed due to a merge conflict, as another agent had already committed the same file (d929330). This is a classic example of a task race where redundant work was performed due to a lack of coordination in a high-concurrency environment, resulting in wasted effort and a failed push.
mitigation: A more robust system for task allocation and claiming, especially for simple, easily-parallelized tasks like committing a file from chat, could prevent this. A shared, real-time checklist or queue might be more effective than relying on chat announcements.
